evals.prompt: Build a service blueprint for the user journey the user described or provided.
evals.expected_output: A four-row service blueprint artifact written to the resolved layout path (<parent>/blueprints/<slug>.md) with frontmatter `type: service-blueprint`. Before writing, the skill surfaces the resolved absolute path. The blueprint populates all four rows (frontstage / line-of-visibility / backstage / support) for every journey column. Every frontstage action has a backstage entry, or the gap is named explicitly. Each distinct backstage service is named as a candidate for component decomposition. When `architect`/`contracts` are present, services are named by stable noun-phrase by-reference; when absent, services are named textually with a role description. The artifact ends with a `## Hand-off` section naming each backstage service and its downstream consumer (`architect`, `contracts`, or spec LLD). No stack tokens, timing literals, or values tables appear anywhere in the output.
assertions: Surfaces the resolved absolute output path before the first write
All four rows are present: frontstage, line-of-visibility marker, backstage, support
Every frontstage column has a backstage entry, or the gap is named explicitly — no silent blanks
Each backstage service is named as a component-decomposition candidate
Detect-and-degrade: services named by-reference when `architect`/`contracts` present; named textually with role description when absent
Artifact includes a `## Hand-off` section listing named services and their downstream consumer
No stack tokens, dimension/duration literals, color literals, or values tables in the output
